title: updates to Glassbox regression
summary:
This week I worked on Glassbox. I updated the fast-path so it keeps the top 5 diverse formulas instead of just one. I rank them using a complexity-penalized score after OLS refit. Now the metadata is consistent across all branches.

I also added logic to route outputs to guided evolution if the model confidence is low or the residual data looks bad. I added a decision helper to track why it routes certain things. For the evolution part, I added sensitivity-aware scaling to the mutations. Finally, I added isotonic regression for the curve classifiers to make calibration better. It was tricky to handle the NaN values in the residuals, but the regression tests now pass.
commitMessages: feat(fast-path): retain top-K diverse candidate formulas and expose them to benchmark routing
feat(sr-benchmark): add adaptive guided-evolution routing from uncertainty and residual risk
feat(evolution): enhance gradient-informed mutation with sensitivity-aware scaling
feat(calibration): implement isotonic regression calibration for curve classifiers
